title: Research
text:
High-resolution climate modeling · Cryosphere dynamics · Glacier mass balance · Permafrost · High mountain hydroclimate · Heatwaves · Human-climate interactions in High Mountain Asia and the contiguous United States
    
    
  



  

    
    Ph.D. Dissertation Work
    
    
      
        High-Resolution Climate Simulations and Impacts of Local Human Factors on Hydroclimate and Glacier Dynamics in High Mountain Asia
      
      
        My dissertation examines how large-scale irrigation across South Asia perturbs the regional hydroclimate system and remote glacier dynamics in the Himalaya and Karakoram, using high-resolution WRF regional climate models.
      
      
        University of Nebraska-Lincoln · Expected Summer 2026
      
    



    
    
      
        🔬 Ongoing Work: Two active projects investigate how irrigation-driven climate changes propagate into moisture transport across South Asia and glacier mass balance in High Mountain Asia. Figures and preliminary results are highlighted on the Home page →
      
    
    
    
      Evaluating the Performance of High-Resolution Climate Simulations over the Central Himalaya and Karakoram
      Gurung, T.R., Chen, L., Ali, S.H., Rowe, C., Arriola, F.M. and Ritzema, R.S.
      Climate Dynamics, 64(3), p.120 · 2026
      
        This study benchmarks WRF simulations against observations across the complex terrain of the Central Himalaya and Karakoram, evaluating their skill in reproducing temperature, precipitation, and atmospheric circulation — a critical step before using these models for impact studies.
      
      DOI: 10.1007/s00382-025-08030-x →
    

    
    
      Understanding the Influence of Soil Moisture on Heatwave Characteristics in the Contiguous United States
      Gurung, T.R. and Chen, L.
      Environmental Research Letters, 19(6) · 2024
      
        Using observational data and model simulations, this paper quantifies how antecedent soil moisture deficits amplify heatwave intensity, duration, and spatial extent across the contiguous US — highlighting land-atmosphere feedbacks as a key driver of extreme heat events.
      
      DOI: 10.1088/1748-9326/ad4dbb →
    

    
    Earlier Research
    
    

      
        
          Glaciology
          Glacier Mass Balance — Rikha Samba
          Long-term mass balance reconstruction (1974–2021) and a decade of in-situ measurements at Rikha Samba Glacier, central Himalaya, using an energy-mass balance model.
          Journal of Glaciology →
        
      

      
        
          Glaciology
          Mass Balances — Yala & Rikha Samba
          Geodetic and glaciological mass balance records (2000–2017) for Yala and Rikha Samba glaciers, providing a long-term dataset for the Nepal Himalaya.
          Earth System Science Data →
        
      

      
        
          CryospherePermafrost
          Permafrost Change in High Mountain Asia
          A comprehensive review of climate change impacts and adaptation strategies related to permafrost dynamics across High Mountain Asia.
          Environ. Res. Letters →
        
      

      
        
          HydroclimateLangtang
          High Mountain Water Cycle — Langtang
          Multi-year observations of the water cycle in the Langtang catchment, Central Himalaya, documenting precipitation, snowmelt, and streamflow dynamics.
          Hydrological Processes →
        
      

    

    
      See all publications →

title: Tika Ram Gurung
text:
Tika Ram Gurung
    Ph.D. Candidate in Climatology & Meteorology
    Department of Earth and Atmospheric Sciences, University of Nebraska-Lincoln


  "Climate and cryosphere scientist with expertise in glacier mass balance, permafrost, high mountain hydroclimate, and heatwaves, now integrating numerical modeling and in-situ field data to understand how soil moisture, irrigation, and moisture transport shape hydroclimate variability from Himalayan glaciers to regional scales."

    
    
      🏔️ Cryosphere
      💧 Hydroclimate
      🌏 High Mountain Asia
      🌾 Irrigation & Climate
      🔥 Heatwaves
      🏜️ Drought
      🖥️ WRF Modeling
      📊 R & Python
    
    
      View Research
      Publications
      CV
      ✉ Contact
    
  




  
    
      
        8+
        Publications
      
      
        20
        Glacier Expeditions
      
      
        6+
        Years Work Experiences
      
      
        2
        Major Awards (2024)
      
    
  




  
    
      About
      
      
        I am a climate scientist and Ph.D. candidate at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln, specializing in land/cryosphere-atmosphere processes and regional climate modeling. My research integrates numerical modeling and large datasets to understand how human factors such as irrigation interact with natural climate systems in South Aisa and High Mountain Asia.
      
      
        Prior to my PhD, I spent several years as a Research Associate and Cryosphere Analyst at ICIMOD, leading glacier monitoring expeditions across high altitude glaciers in the Central Himalaya.
      
    
    
      Ongoing Research
      
  
        
          
          
            HydroclimateWRF
            Irrigation & South Asian Hydroclimate
            Quantifying how large-scale irrigation in the Indo-Gangetic Plain alters moisture flux and precipitation patterns across South Asia.
          
        
        
          
          
            Glacier mass balanceCryosphere
            Irrigation & Glacier Dynamics
            Investigating the cascading effects of irrigation-driven climate modification on glacier mass balance in High Mountain Asia.
